market_overview.headline: Market structure deteriorating with both SPY and QQQ showing bearish EMA stacks, while 37% of stocks are in full bear mode.
market_overview.detail: Only 26% of stocks remain in full bull structures vs 37% in full bear, with both major indices below their weekly EMAs and showing crossover stress signals. BTC's full bear structure confirms risk-off sentiment.

reversal_candidates.headline: Bear Rally Reversal Setups (Bullish Crossover Alerts)

momentum_themes.headline: Electronic Technology Leading Momentum Surge
momentum_themes.detail: Electronic technology dominates momentum with memory/storage plays leading (SNDK +49%, WDC +29%). Optical networking and semiconductor equipment also accelerating hard.

sector_analysis.headline: Energy Minerals Dominate While Financials Collapse

strongest.note: Perfect bullish sweep with all 15 energy stocks in full bull mode, averaging +18% above 21W EMAs.
Strong infrastructure/services theme with 82% bullish participation and solid momentum.
Manufacturing showing resilience with two-thirds maintaining bullish structure despite broader weakness.

weakest.note: Complete sector breakdown with all 5 stocks in bearish structure, averaging -7.4% below 21W EMAs.
Tech services carnage continues with 82% in bearish mode, mega-caps leading the decline.
Financial sector collapse with 81% bearish, banks and insurers hit hardest in the selloff.

sector_analysis.detail: Clear rotation from growth/tech into energy and industrial plays. Financials getting crushed suggests rate concerns, while energy's perfect score reflects commodity strength.

items.text: Both SPY and QQQ below all EMAs with crossover stress signals — broad market structure deteriorating rapidly
37% of stocks in full bear mode vs only 26% full bull — worst breadth reading suggests more downside ahead
High volume pullbacks in LHX (1.64x avg) signal potential institutional distribution — avoid high-vol setups
BTC's full bear structure (-45% from fair value) confirms risk-off environment — crypto correlation risk elevated
